title: Saint Catherine Parish

infobox.value: ([Jamaican Patois](/wiki/Jamaican_Patois "Jamaican Patois"): *Sint Cyatrine*) is a [parish](/wiki/Parishes_of_Jamaica "Parishes of Jamaica") in the [south east](/wiki/Ordinal_directions "Ordinal directions") of [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica"). It is located in the [county](/wiki/Counties_of_Jamaica "Counties of Jamaica") of Middlesex, and is one of the island's largest and most economically valued parishes because of its many resources. It includes the first [capital](/wiki/Capital_(political) "Capital (political)") of Jamaica, [Spanish Town](/wiki/Spanish_Town "Spanish Town"), originally known as San Jago de la Vega or Santiago de la Vega (St. James of the Plain).

Parish of Jamaica

**Saint Catherine** ([Jamaican Patois](/wiki/Jamaican_Patois "Jamaican Patois"): *Sint Cyatrine*) is a [parish](/wiki/Parishes_of_Jamaica "Parishes of Jamaica") in the [south east](/wiki/Ordinal_directions "Ordinal directions") of [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica"). It is located in the [county](/wiki/Counties_of_Jamaica "Counties of Jamaica") of Middlesex, and is one of the island's largest and most economically valued parishes because of its many resources. It includes the first [capital](/wiki/Capital_(political) "Capital (political)") of Jamaica, [Spanish Town](/wiki/Spanish_Town "Spanish Town"), originally known as San Jago de la Vega or Santiago de la Vega (St. James of the Plain).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The modern boundaries of St. Catherine were decided in 1867 when four smaller parishes were amalgamated.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The historic parishes of [Saint Dorothy Parish](/wiki/Saint_Dorothy_Parish,_Jamaica "Saint Dorothy Parish, Jamaica"), [Saint John Parish](/wiki/Saint_John_Parish,_Jamaica "Saint John Parish, Jamaica") and [Saint Thomas in the Vale Parish, Jamaica](/wiki/Saint_Thomas_in_the_Vale_Parish,_Jamaica "Saint Thomas in the Vale Parish, Jamaica") were merged with the historic parish of Saint Catherine.[[4]](#cite_note-JPR-4)

## Geography and People

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography and People")]

St Catherine is located at [18°N 77°W﻿ / ﻿18°N 77°W﻿ / 18; -77](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Saint_Catherine_Parish&params=18_N_77_W_type:adm1st). It is bordered by [St Andrew](/wiki/Saint_Andrew,_Jamaica "Saint Andrew, Jamaica") in the east, [Clarendon](/wiki/Clarendon,_Jamaica "Clarendon, Jamaica") in the west, and by [St Mary](/wiki/Saint_Mary,_Jamaica "Saint Mary, Jamaica") and [St Ann](/wiki/Saint_Ann,_Jamaica "Saint Ann, Jamaica") in the north. It has an area of 1,192 km2, making it one of Jamaica's largest parishes and it is one of the fastest growing parishes in the nation and has the largest economy out of all fourteen parishes. Except for the Hellshire Hills near the coast, the south of the parish is virtually flat. The central and northern sections are very mountainous, however; the northern border is on Mount Rosser, which crosses over into [St Ann](/wiki/Saint_Ann,_Jamaica "Saint Ann, Jamaica"), the highest point being 686 metres (2,251 ft).

A [plain](/wiki/Plain "Plain") of approximately 230 square kilometres (57,000 acres) occupies the southern part of the Rio Cobre [basin](/wiki/Drainage_basin "Drainage basin"). The Rio Cobre is the only river that runs along the southern plain. It provides water to [irrigate](/wiki/Irrigation "Irrigation") over 73 square kilometres (18,000 acres) on the plain.

## Commerce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Commerce")]

### Development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Development")]

Out of all the parishes (with the exception of [Kingston Parish](/wiki/Kingston_Parish "Kingston Parish") and [Saint Andrew Parish](/wiki/Saint_Andrew_Parish,_Jamaica "Saint Andrew Parish, Jamaica")) Saint Catherine shows the most potential for urban development. With its good water resources, virtually flat landscape, and nearness to the capital, it could possibly be one of the Caribbean's major urban areas in the next few years.

### Agriculture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Agriculture")]

Agriculture remains the main source of [employment](/wiki/Employment "Employment") in the parish. There are many small farmers who practice mixed farming; [crops](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture") such as [bananas](/wiki/Banana "Banana"), [coconuts](/wiki/Coconut "Coconut"), [pineapple](/wiki/Pineapple "Pineapple"), [citrus](/wiki/Citrus "Citrus"), [pumpkins](/wiki/Pumpkin "Pumpkin"), [peppers](/wiki/Capsicum "Capsicum"), [coffee](/wiki/Coffee "Coffee") and [callaloo](/wiki/Callaloo "Callaloo") are planted for both domestic and commercial purposes. The larger properties produce [sugar cane](/wiki/Sugar_cane "Sugar cane"), [bananas](/wiki/Banana "Banana") and [citrus](/wiki/Citrus "Citrus") mainly for export. Dairy farms are also found in the parish. One of these is a 4.0 square kilometres (1,000 acres) [farm](/wiki/Farm "Farm") in Old Harbour. The Salt Ponds District between Spanish Town, Port Henderson and Passage Fort is noted for the fine fish especially calipers.

### Manufacturing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Manufacturing")]

St Catherine is second only to Kingston as an industrial center. Industrial plants are some of the biggest employers in the parish. [Spanish Town](/wiki/Spanish_Town "Spanish Town") has the largest salt producing plant in the Caribbean, while Jamaica Milk Products, an affiliate of the [Nestlé](/wiki/Nestl%C3%A9 "Nestlé") organization, has a factory in [Bog Walk](/wiki/Bog_Walk "Bog Walk"), another major town in the parish. The largest [power plant](/wiki/Power_plant "Power plant") in the island and several factories are located in Old Harbour. Twickenham Park, near [Spanish Town](/wiki/Spanish_Town "Spanish Town"), is another industrial estate with mainly light industries including [cigarettes](/wiki/Cigarettes "Cigarettes"), [carpets](/wiki/Carpets "Carpets"), [batteries](/wiki/Battery_(electricity) "Battery (electricity)"), [ackee](/wiki/Plastic "Plastic"), [plastic](/wiki/Plastic "Plastic") items, [medical](/wiki/Medical "Medical") and [pharmaceutical](/wiki/Pharmaceutical "Pharmaceutical") products.

### Tourist sites

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Tourist sites")]

* Jamaica's [emancipation](/wiki/Abolitionism_in_the_United_Kingdom "Abolitionism in the United Kingdom") Square can be found in Spanish Town. This is the only [Georgian](/wiki/Georgian_architecture "Georgian architecture") square in [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica"). Kings House and the [House of Assembly](/wiki/House_of_Assembly "House of Assembly") on the west and east sides, respectively, were erected in 1762. The [Courthouse](/wiki/Courthouse "Courthouse") was built in 1819 and used as a [chapel](/wiki/Chapel "Chapel") and [armory](/wiki/Armory_(military) "Armory (military)") with the Town Hall upstairs.
* The Rio Cobre River and [Gorge](/wiki/Gorge "Gorge") is one of the largest in the island. Sinking at a place called River Sink at Worthy Park, it runs underground for nearly six kilometres, then surfaces at River Head Grand [Cave](/wiki/Cave "Cave") in St Thomas-in-the-Vale. Before entering the [gorge](/wiki/Gorge "Gorge") it is joined by a number of [tributaries](/wiki/Tributaries "Tributaries"), such as the Thomas River, the Rio D'Oro and the Rio Pedro. In the early 1770s, a road was opened through the gorge. The [Flat Bridge](/wiki/Flat_Bridge "Flat Bridge") was originally constructed of [logs](/wiki/Logging "Logging"), which were washed away in a flood. The present bridge was built and it has withstood countless floods. A marker can be found, which shows where the water rose to over 25 feet (7.6 m) above the bridge in 1933. An apocryphal story is that at noon, on every [Good Friday](/wiki/Good_Friday "Good Friday"), for a short period of time the [ghosts](/wiki/Ghosts "Ghosts") of all the slaves who drowned in the river can be seen.
* Caymanas Race Track, Jamaica's most famous race track, is in St Catherine.
* St Clair Cave, one of Jamaica's more famous caves, is found in the parish. This large, complex cave contains one of the most notable bat roosts on the island, and is one of the few known historical sites for the possibly extinct bat, *[Phyllonycteris aphylla](/wiki/Phyllonycteris_aphylla "Phyllonycteris aphylla")*. Because of the biological importance of the system, and the inherent dangers involved in exploring it (i.e. noxious gases, [histoplasmosis](/wiki/Histoplasmosis "Histoplasmosis"), [Cutaneous larva migrans](/wiki/Cutaneous_larva_migrans "Cutaneous larva migrans"), difficult footing), casual visitation is discouraged.
* Hellshire Beach is located on the south coast of Jamaica in St. Catherine Parish about 20 minutes away from Kingston. Not a tourist spot due to its distance from major hotels.

## Government and infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Government and infrastructure")]

The Rio Cobre Correctional Centre of the [Department of Correctional Services, Jamaica](/wiki/Department_of_Correctional_Services,_Jamaica "Department of Correctional Services, Jamaica") is located in [Spanish Town](/wiki/Spanish_Town "Spanish Town"), Saint Catherine Parish.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Politics")]

Saint Catherine Parish is represented in the [Parliament of Jamaica](/wiki/Parliament_of_Jamaica "Parliament of Jamaica") by eleven [single-member constituencies](/wiki/Constituencies_of_Jamaica "Constituencies of Jamaica"):

* [Saint Catherine Central](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_Central "Saint Catherine Central")
* [Saint Catherine East Central](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_East_Central "Saint Catherine East Central")
* [Saint Catherine Eastern](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_Eastern "Saint Catherine Eastern")
* [Saint Catherine North Central](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_North_Central "Saint Catherine North Central")
* [Saint Catherine North Eastern](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_North_Eastern "Saint Catherine North Eastern")
* [Saint Catherine North Western](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_North_Western "Saint Catherine North Western")
* [Saint Catherine South Central](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_South_Central "Saint Catherine South Central")
* [Saint Catherine South Eastern](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_South_Eastern "Saint Catherine South Eastern")
* [Saint Catherine South Western](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_South_Western "Saint Catherine South Western")
* [Saint Catherine Southern](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_Southern "Saint Catherine Southern")
* [Saint Catherine West Central](/wiki/Saint_Catherine_West_Central_(Jamaica_Parliament_constituency) "Saint Catherine West Central (Jamaica Parliament constituency)")

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Catherine_Parish&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: References")]

1. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-cathe_1-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-cathe_1-1) The title of largest parish in Jamaica has caused some confusion. The parishes of St. Catherine, Saint Elizabeth or St. Ann are usually ranked as the largest, depending on the source. *The Gleaner*, one of the nations most reputable newspapers, lists St. Catherine as the largest with an area of 1,260 sq km, St. Ann as the second largest with 1200 sq km, and St. Elizabeth third with 1,185 sq km ([Geography and History of Jamaica](http://www.discoverjamaica.com/gleaner/discover/geography/polgol.htm). *The Jamaica Gleaner*. Retrieved 9 April 2009). However, The Jamaica Library Service database ranks St Ann the largest with an area of 1212.6 sq km ([Saint Ann](http://www.jamlib.org.jm/stann_history.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20081204101122/http://www.jamlib.org.jm/stann_history.htm) 4 December 2008 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"). *Jamaica Library Services*. Retrieved 9 April 2009), St. Elizabeth second with 1212.4 sq km ([St. Elizabeth](http://www.jamlib.org.jm/steliza_history.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20081204113738/http://www.jamlib.org.jm/steliza_history.htm) 4 December 2008 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"). *Jamaica Library Services*. Retrieved 9 April 2009), and St. Catherine third with 1192.4 sq km ([Saint Catherine](http://www.jamlib.org.jm/stcathe_history.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20081204114219/http://www.jamlib.org.jm/stcathe_history.htm) 4 December 2008 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"). *Jamaica Library Services*. Retrieved 9 April 2009).
2. **[^](#cite_ref-population_2-0)** ["Population by Parish"](http://statinja.gov.jm/Demo_SocialStats/populationbyparish.aspx). *Statistical Institute of Jamaica*. Retrieved 16 September 2015.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** ["Parish Profile: St. Catherine"](https://jis.gov.jm/information/parish-profiles/parish-profile-st-catherine/). *Jamaica Information Service*. Retrieved 27 September 2019.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-JPR_4-0)** ["Jamaican Parish Reference"](https://web.archive.org/web/20091030212422/http://prestwidge.com/river/jamaicanparishes.html). Prestwidges of Jamaica. 30 October 2009. Archived from [the original](http://prestwidge.com/river/jamaicanparishes.html) on 30 October 2009. Retrieved 13 February 2022.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** "[JUVENILE CORRECTIONAL CENTRES](https://web.archive.org/web/20110614100020/http://www.dcsj.net/dcsj/juveniles.htm)." [Department of Correctional Services, Jamaica](/wiki/Department_of_Correctional_Services,_Jamaica "Department of Correctional Services, Jamaica"). Retrieved on 2 October 2010.
